briefs.title: Council to Consider: Earthquake Preparedness Presentation from UC Berkeley Scientists
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to receive a presentation from UC Berkeley scientists and local emergency groups about the earthquake risk posed by the Hayward Fault, which has a high chance of producing a major quake in the coming decades. No new rules or spending are being decided yet — any future policy or funding recommendations will come back to Council separately in fall 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for fall 2026 Council updates — future earthquake policy could affect your business operations.

key_dates.label: Richmond Council earthquake preparedness presentation — meeting date

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to Vote on Opposing CO2 Pipeline and Carbon Storage Project Near Richmond Refinery
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a resolution opposing the Montezuma Carbon Hub — a proposed project that would build CO2 pipelines through Richmond and nearby counties to capture and store carbon waste from industrial sites like the Chevron Refinery. Councilmember Claudia Jimenez argues the project puts nearby communities at greater health and environmental risk without meaningfully reducing climate pollution.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 28 — it could affect energy and refinery operations in the region.

key_dates.label: Richmond Council vote — Montezuma CO2 pipeline opposition resolution

briefs.title: Council to Consider Exploring Park Development in Richmond's Pullman Neighborhood
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council will consider directing staff to look into acquiring a vacant lot at South 27th Street and Florida Avenue to potentially build the Pullman neighborhood's first nearby public park. No funding is being requested yet — staff would have 90 days to report back on whether buying the land is feasible and what funding options exist.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — a new park could affect nearby property values and foot traffic.

key_dates.label: Staff report due — Pullman park feasibility and timeline (90-day deadline)

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail

briefs.title: Council to vote on $299,797 contract to plan relocation of historic WWII ship
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on approving a $299,797 contract with Liftech Consultants, Inc. to plan and begin designing the relocation of the SS Red Oak Victory — a historic WWII-era cargo ship — to a new berth near the city's ferry terminal and a national park visitor centre. The full move is estimated to cost $16–$20 million, and this contract would fund early-stage planning and engineering work through June 30, 2027.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it could open Basin No. 5 to new commercial maritime tenants.

key_dates.label: Liftech Consultants planning contract term end — SS Red Oak Victory relocation

briefs.industries: Transportation & Logistics, Construction & Development, Professional Services

briefs.title: Proposed: Richmond Council to vote on updated sidewalk vendor rules and enforcement
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed ordinance that would update the rules for sidewalk vendors — people who sell goods or food from carts on public sidewalks. The changes would add a formal waiver process (a way to request an exception to certain rules), clarify how permits are applied for and can be denied or revoked, and bring the city's rules in line with new California state laws protecting vendor privacy and ability to pay fines.
briefs.action_required: If you operate as a sidewalk vendor in Richmond, watch for the council vote and review the updated permit and enforcement rules.

key_dates.label: Richmond sidewalk vendor ordinance amendment — first reading council meeting

briefs.title: Council to Consider: BART Presentation on Transit Cuts Threatening Richmond Commuters
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to receive a presentation from BART Director Barnali Ghosh on April 28, 2026, covering recent service improvements and a serious funding crisis. Without new voter-approved funding, BART could cut service by up to 70%, close stations early at 9 p.m., raise fares by 50%, and lay off over 1,000 workers — starting in 2027.
briefs.action_required: Watch the November 2026 ballot for a regional transit funding measure that could prevent major BART service cuts.

key_dates.label: California regional transit funding measure — November 2026 ballot vote
BART Alternative Service Plan potential cuts take effect — FY2027

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Proposed Updates to Sidewalk Vendor Rules in Richmond
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proposed ordinance (a local law) that would update Chapter 7.42 of the city's Municipal Code — the rules governing sidewalk vendors. The changes would update administrative procedures, exemptions (who doesn't need to follow certain rules), and enforcement mechanisms (how the rules are applied). This is a first reading, meaning Council will consider it but a final vote has not yet been taken.
briefs.action_required: Review the proposed sidewalk vendor ordinance changes and attend the April 28 meeting to comment.

key_dates.label: Richmond sidewalk vendor ordinance amendment — first reading (public hearing)

briefs.title: Council to Consider Hiring Consultant to Guide Community Input on Limited-Term Revenue Spending
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on authorizing the City Manager and City Attorney to negotiate and sign a contract with Dalberg Advisors, a consulting firm selected through a competitive bidding process. The consultant would lead community engagement — meaning public outreach and input-gathering — to help shape how the city holds and spends money from limited-term revenue sources.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision in May/June 2026 — it may shape how city funds are spent in your community.

key_dates.label: Richmond Council contract award for Dalberg Advisors — expected decision

briefs.title: Council to Consider Contract for Community Engagement on New Limited-Term Revenue
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on approving a contract with Dalberg Advisors to help gather community input on 'limited-term revenue' — meaning temporary new taxes or fees the city may pursue. Council will also receive an update on engagement efforts already underway related to this potential new funding.
briefs.action_required: Attend the April 28, 2026 budget session to share your views on proposed new city revenue measures.

key_dates.label: Richmond City Council budget session — limited-term revenue contract vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on $250,000 contract for wastewater operator procurement advisor
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on hiring The Halleman Group, LLC as an 'Owner's Advisor' — an outside expert who helps the city run a fair hiring process — to find a new operator for its wastewater and stormwater systems. The proposed contract would cost up to $250,000 and run from May 2026 to April 2028, helping the city replace its current operator, Veolia, before their contract expires in May 2027.
briefs.action_required: Monitor this decision if your business depends on wastewater or stormwater services in Richmond.

key_dates.label: Proposed Owner's Advisor contract start date — Richmond wastewater procurement
Veolia wastewater operations contract expiry — Richmond
Proposed Owner's Advisor contract end date — Richmond wastewater procurement

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Natural Resources, Professional Services

briefs.title: Council to vote on $250K contract for wastewater and stormwater operator advisory services
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on approving a contract worth up to $250,000 with The Halleman Group, LLC. The firm would act as an 'Owner's Advisor' — meaning an independent expert hired to guide the city — to help find and hire a private operator to run the city's wastewater treatment plant and stormwater collection systems from May 2026 to April 2028.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — this could affect businesses in the wastewater and utilities sector.

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Professional Services, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to vote on appointing Eva Hernandez to Richmond Workforce Development Board
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on appointing Eva Hernandez to a vacant seat on the Workforce Development Board, with a term running until March 1, 2030. Hernandez is a Richmond resident and Chevron public affairs representative with a background in community engagement and leadership.

briefs.title: Council to Consider Appointing Bryan M. Harris to County Aging Advisory Council
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council will consider appointing Bryan M. Harris to the City of Richmond's seat on the Contra Costa County Advisory Council on Aging (ACOA). If approved, his term would run until September 30, 2028. Harris brings over 25 years of healthcare experience and a Master's degree in Gerontology, and currently serves on the Richmond Commission on Aging.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Appointing Ric Ambrose to Richmond Arts and Culture Commission
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on appointing Ric Ambrose to a vacant seat on the Richmond Arts and Culture Commission, with a term running to January 31, 2030. Ambrose brings over 30 years of arts leadership experience, including directing art centres and curating more than 300 exhibitions. There is no financial impact to the city.

briefs.title: Council to vote on: Creating a Richmond–Sebastia Sister City Commission
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on establishing a formal commission to manage the city's sister city relationship with Sebastia, Palestine — a partnership Council approved in August 2025. If adopted, the commission would be responsible for planning cultural, educational, and economic exchanges, and would be directed to write its own bylaws (internal rules), which Council would then need to approve before they take effect.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the Council decision on April 28, 2026 — this is a civic initiative with no direct business compliance requirements.

briefs.title: Council to consider proclaiming May 3–9, 2026 as National Small Business Week in Richmond
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proclamation recognizing May 3–9, 2026 as National Small Business Week. If approved, the City would officially celebrate local small businesses and encourage residents to support them, with activities planned from May 4–8, 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for local events May 4–8 and promote your business during National Small Business Week.

key_dates.label: National Small Business Week — Richmond City activities begin
National Small Business Week — Richmond City activities end

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Proclaiming April 2026 as Sexual Assault Awareness Month in Richmond
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proclamation declaring April 2026 as Sexual Assault Awareness Month. The proclamation would signal the city's commitment to raising public awareness about sexual assault, supporting survivors, and promoting prevention and education in the community.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Proclaiming April 2026 Alcohol Awareness Month in Richmond
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proclamation recognizing April 2026 as Alcohol Awareness Month. If approved, the proclamation would encourage schools, families, businesses, and community groups to promote education, prevention, and support services related to alcohol use — with a focus on reducing youth substance misuse.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — businesses in hospitality or liquor may want to align community messaging.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: National Small Business Week Proclamation & Sister Cities Commission
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on several items from the Mayor's Office, including a proclamation recognizing May 3–9, 2026 as National Small Business Week and encouraging residents to support local businesses. Council will also consider establishing a Richmond-Sebastia Sister Cities Commission and approving several board appointments — all on the consent calendar, meaning they are expected to pass together without debate.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the National Small Business Week proclamation — use it to promote your business to the local community May 3–9.

key_dates.label: National Small Business Week — Richmond proclamation period begins
National Small Business Week — Richmond proclamation period ends
Richmond City Council meeting — Mayor's Office consent items vote

briefs.title: Council to vote on $3.275M contract amendment and management deal for Harbour-8 Park
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a fourth contract amendment with Pogo Park, a local nonprofit, that would release $3,275,000 in previously approved funding to finish building Harbour-8 Park — bringing the total contract value to $12,430,725. Council will also consider authorizing a long-term Operations and Management Agreement with Pogo Park to run the park, community hall, and related facilities through May 2029, with two possible one-year extensions.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 28, 2026 — it could affect park programming and community facility access.

key_dates.label: Richmond Council vote — Harbour-8 Park contract amendment and management agreement
Pogo Park Operations and Management Agreement proposed end date

briefs.title: Council to vote on accepting $60,000 grant for Richmond youth outdoor camping program
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on accepting a $60,000 grant from the California Tahoe Conservancy's 'Tahoe for All' program. The funds would support the Pathfinders: Youth Outdoors Richmond Weekend Camping Program, which offers free three-day camping trips, hiking, and kayaking at Lake Tahoe for local youth over three years.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 28, 2026 — no direct business impact expected.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $390,000 to keep Richmond's electric bikeshare program running
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a third contract amendment with Charleston Mobility (also known as Ride Today) worth up to $390,000, bringing the total contract to $3,540,341. The money would keep the city's electric bikeshare program operating through the end of the current fiscal year while staff decide whether to continue, expand, or shut down the program in the 2026–27 budget process.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — it will determine whether the e-bike program continues or winds down.

key_dates.label: Richmond e-bikeshare future — council budget decision expected for FY2026-27

briefs.title: Council to vote on: $42,000 courier contract to keep library book-sharing program running
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on approving a sole-source contract — meaning only one supplier qualifies, so no competitive bidding is required — with Unity Courier Service, Inc. to deliver borrowed library materials between the Richmond Public Library and 70+ partner libraries across California and Nevada. The proposed three-year deal runs July 1, 2025 to June 30, 2028, at $14,000 per year, for a total of $42,000.
briefs.action_required: No direct action needed — monitor the April 28, 2026 council decision if your business uses library services.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $29,000 contract extension for labour compliance tracking software
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a $29,000 amendment to its contract with LCPtracker, Inc., a software platform that helps the city track whether contractors on city-funded projects are following local hiring rules and paying workers correctly. If approved, the total contract value would reach just under $57,000, running through March 4, 2027.
briefs.action_required: If you bid on Richmond city-funded projects, watch for this decision — it affects how payroll and hiring compliance is tracked.

key_dates.label: LCPtracker contract amendment — Richmond Council vote date
LCPtracker amended contract term ends — Richmond labour compliance software

briefs.title: Council to vote on e-bike share extension, labour compliance software, and library courier contract
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on several items under its Library and Community Services consent calendar, including a $390,000 contract extension to keep the city's electric bike-share program running, a $29,000 amendment to renew labour compliance tracking software (LCPtracker) for city-funded projects, and a $42,000 sole-source courier contract for inter-library book delivery. These items are on the consent calendar, meaning they are expected to be approved together without separate debate unless a council member or member of the public requests otherwise.
briefs.action_required: Attend the April 28, 2026 council meeting or submit written comments before 1:00 p.m. that day to have your say.

briefs.title: Council to vote on $112,900 contract for Port of Richmond economic impact study
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on hiring John C. Martin Associates, LLC to conduct a full economic impact study of the Port of Richmond — measuring jobs, income, business revenue, and tax contributions from port activity. The contract would cost up to $112,900 and run until June 30, 2027, with results used to guide port planning, policy decisions, and future grant applications.
briefs.action_required: If your business depends on the Port of Richmond, watch for this study — it may shape future port policy and investment.

key_dates.label: Port of Richmond economic impact study contract end date

briefs.title: Council to vote on waste management partnership agreement to meet California organics law
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on approving a formal agreement (called a Memorandum of Understanding, or MOU) with RecycleMore, the regional waste authority, to share responsibilities for meeting California's SB 1383 organic waste rules. The law requires cities to divert 75% of organic waste from landfills and recover edible food — and a state audit of Richmond is expected in 2026.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — if you generate organic waste or food surplus, SB 1383 compliance rules may apply to your business.

key_dates.label: SB 1383 compliance audit of Richmond by CalRecycle expected
Richmond City Council vote on SB 1383 MOU with RecycleMore

briefs.title: Council to Consider Authorizing $32M Grant Application for Richmond Affordable Housing and Bike Infrastructure
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on joining a roughly $32 million grant application under California's Affordable Housing and Sustainable Communities (AHSC) Program. If approved, the City would partner with two housing non-profits to support the Riveter Project — a 59-unit affordable housing development — and would be responsible for building new bike lanes and pedestrian walkway improvements using any grant funds awarded.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision — bike and pedestrian infrastructure near your business may be affected.

key_dates.label: AHSC grant application deadline — Richmond Riveter Project submission closes

briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Transportation & Logistics

briefs.title: Council to vote on $300,000 grant for Richmond Tiny House Village youth housing project
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on approving a grant agreement of up to $300,000 with Hope Solutions to fund construction costs and on-site support services for the Tiny House Village at 175 23rd Street — a seven-unit housing project for formerly unhoused youth aged 18–24. The grant would run from April 1, 2026 to December 31, 2027, replacing an earlier agreement whose deadline expired before it could be signed.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on April 28, 2026 — this affects affordable housing funding in Richmond.

key_dates.label: Richmond Tiny House Village grant agreement term end date

No new rules or requirements are created by this vote.

briefs.title: Council to Consider: Proclaiming May 2026 as AAPI Heritage Month in Richmond
briefs.summary: Richmond City Council is set to vote on a proclamation declaring May 2026 as Asian American and Pacific Islander (AAPI) Heritage Month.
